title: inula britannica var. chinensis
intro: Inula britannica var. chinensis, also known as Chinese Inula, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae, and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Chinese Inula include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and alleviating digestive issues. Chinese Inula is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, where it is combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
